title: castilleja miniata subsp. dixonii
intro: Castilleja miniata subsp. dixonii, also known as Dixon's Indian paintbrush, is a flowering plant species native to the western United States. It belongs to the family Orobanchaceae and is known for its bright red and yellow flowers, which are pollinated by bees and butterflies. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Castilleja miniata subsp. dixonii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to promote overall health and well-being.
